content.full_text:
BRADLEY W. HERTZ
ATTORNEY AT LAW
15223 MAGNOLIA BOULEVARD, SUITE A
SHERMAN OAKS, CALIFORNIA 91403
TELEPHONE (818) 789-3322

January 6, 1992

Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Proper Name and Forms for a Controlled Committee
Which is Primarily Formed to Oppose the Qualification
and Passage of a Local Ballot Measure (Recall)

Dear Jay:

Pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b),1 this letter
requests confirmation of the telephone advice you gave me today
regarding the proper committee name and the proper campaign
statement form for a controlled committee which is primarily
formed to oppose a recall effort in a special district. I am
requesting informal assistance as a person with a duty to advise
other persons relating to their actions under the Political
Reform Act (2 California Code of Regulations, Section 18329 (c)).

Facts

An officeholder forms and controls a committee for the
purpose of opposing the qualification and passage of a recall
effort against her. The officeholder's name does not appear in
the committee's name. Also, the committee's name does not
contain the words "a committee against Proposition (or Measure)
__." The committee files its required campaign reports using
the Form 419 (Ballot Measure Committee Campaign Statement - Long
Form).

Questions Presented

1. As a controlled committee, must the committee name
include the name of the controlling officeholder?

2. As a recipient committee primarily formed to oppose the
qualification and passage of a local ballot measure (a recall),
whose principal activity is to oppose a ballot measure
(a recall), must the committee name include the words "a
committee against Proposition (or Measure) __" (or in this
case, "a committee against the recall of __")?

___________________

1 All statutory references are to the California Government
Code, unless otherwise specified.

Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 6, 1992
Page Two


     3.     Is the completed Form 419 the proper form for the
committee to use in filing its required campaign reports?

                              Discussion

     As a controlled committee (Section 82016) which was
primarily formed (Section 82047.5) to oppose the qualification
and passage of a local ballot measure (a recall), it would seem
that Information Manual D, in conjunction with the Act and
Regulations, would be used to determine what the committee should
properly call itself.

     Information Manual D (along with a Form 419) was sent to
me by the Registrar/Clerk of the County in which the recall is
pending, to assist in accurately completing the committee's
reports.

     Information Manual D is entitled:

     "Campaign Provisions of the Political Reform Act For:
Recipient Committees Primarily Formed to Support or Oppose
the Qualification or Passage of a State or Local Ballot
Measure (Including Committees Controlled By A Candidate)."

     Information Manual D, at page 33, states as follows:

     "NAME IDENTIFICATION REQUIREMENTS FOR COMMITTEES

     "Ballot Measure Committees

          "In addition to the requirements for "controlled"
and "sponsored" recipient committees as set out below,
any committee whose principal activity is to support or
oppose a ballot measure must include the statement "a
committee for Proposition (or Measure) ___ or "a
committee against Proposition (or Measure) ___ " or
equivalent language, in any reference to the committee
under the Political Reform Act.  The committee's full
name must be used on the committee's Statement of
Organization (Form 410), its campaign statements, and
its mass mailings...."

Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 6, 1992
Page Three

"Controlled Ballot Measure Committees

"The name of any committee must include or be
accompanied by the name of any individual, entity, or
other person by which the committee is controlled...."

According to the above information set forth in Information
Manual D, the name of the committee described in the Facts above
should contain the name of the controlling officeholder and
should contain the words, "a committee against the recall of
__________." See also Section 84107.

When I asked you if the committee is considered both a
controlled committee and a primarily formed committee and if both
of these name requirements were in effect, you replied that the
committee should not be considered both a controlled committee
and a primarily formed committee, but should be considered only a
controlled committee. Accordingly, you stated that the words "a
committee against the recall of __________" would not be required.

You further stated that the requirement that a controlled
committee's name include the name of the controlling officeholder
is no longer in effect, as a result of Proposition 68 being held
inapplicable.

Based on your advice, therefore, the name of the committee
described in the Facts above would not need to include the
controlling officeholder's name or the words "a committee against
the recall of __________.

As to the proper campaign statement form to use, Information
Manual D states, on the back of the cover page, that:

"A controlled committee 'primarily formed' to
support or oppose a local ballot measure or
the qualification of a local ballot measure
must file campaign disclosure reports at the
same times and in the same location(s) that
the controlling candidate or officeholder
must file his or her regular semi-annual and
pre-election campaign reports as specified in
Information Manual A. In addition, the
committee must file reports as a ballot
measure committee and must follow the
instructions in Information Manual D.
Ballot Measure Committees should file
campaign statements using Form 420,
not Form 490"(emphasis added).

Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 6, 1992
Page Four


According to the Registrar/Clerk, and as you mentioned, the
committee should file its campaign statements on the Form 419,
and not the Form 420.

Please confirm that my understanding of our conversation and
your advice was correct. If not, please advise me otherwise, so
that I may advise my client as to the proper name for the
committee and the proper campaign statements to file.
Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Bradley W. Hertz
